title: The largest oil supply shock since World War II
subtitle: Approximate oil disruptions and available global spare capacity, % of world supply
source: Rapidan Energy Group, EIA, BP, St. Louis Fed, US Senate, JP Morgan Asset Management, March 9, 2026
editorialPoint: Previous crises disrupted 5-10% of global supply with 5-35% spare capacity available. The Hormuz closure has disrupted 20% of supply with effectively 0% spare capacity — because the spare capacity sits behind the closed strait.
note: The 2026 crisis is uniquely dangerous because it combines the largest supply disruption with the smallest effective spare capacity. OPEC has ~4.4M b/d of spare capacity, but 75%+ of it is located in countries whose export infrastructure runs through the closed strait.
